title: No result for apt install/search podman on RaspPi 4B running 4 DietPi 64bit
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**
Running DietPi 7.1.2 64bit on a Raspberry Pi 4B 4GB (arm64). Using 'apt search podman' or 'apt install podman' (after an 'apt search'), I can't find the package. I have flushed my firewall and have had no problem finding other packages before. 

My apt sources look like this:
deb https://deb.debian.org/debian/ buster main contrib non-free
deb https://deb.debian.org/debian/ buster-updates main contrib non-free
deb https://deb.debian.org/debian-security/ buster/updates main contrib non-free
deb https://deb.debian.org/debian/ buster-backports main contrib non-free
deb https://archive.raspberrypi.org/debian/ buster main
<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Install & [DietPi 64bit Raspberry Pi image](https://dietpi.com/downloads/images/DietPi_RPi-ARMv8-Buster.7z) onto Raspberry Pi 4B

2. apt update && apt upgrade

3. apt search podman/apt install podman

**Describe the results you received:**
apt search podman: 
Sorting... Done
Full Text Search... Done

apt install podman: 
E: Unable to locate package podman

**Describe the results you expected:**
I expected apt to be able to find the podman package, to which I could then install onto my system.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
I was going to try and install podman manually using [this guide](https://computingforgeeks.com/how-to-install-podman-on-debian/), although I wanted to first troubleshoot why I couldn't find the package in the first place.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
N/A
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
N/A
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
Listing... Done
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
[DietPi 7.1.2 64bit](https://dietpi.com/downloads/images/DietPi_RPi-ARMv8-Buster.7z), Raspberry Pi 4B 4GB (arm64)

uname -a:
Linux hostname 5.10.17-v8+ #1414 SMP PREEMPT Fri Apr 30 13:23:25 BST 2021 aarch64 GNU/Linux

cat /etc/debian-version:
10.9

comments.body: As far as I know podman is available for Bullseye

I'm not sure why podman isn't available on my system. I've included below the output of 'uname -a' and my Debian version, and have edited the post with this new info.

uname -a:
Linux hostname 5.10.17-v8+ #1414 SMP PREEMPT Fri Apr 30 13:23:25 BST 2021 aarch64 GNU/Linux

cat /etc/debian-version:
10.9

Look here:
https://packages.debian.org/bullseye/podman

You've got Buster:
deb https://deb.debian.org/debian/ buster main contrib non-free

and you need at least Bullseye
I'm a bit lost here sorry, do I need to reinstall my OS to Bullseye in order to use podman?

You can upgrade your system to Bullseye or do what you wanted to do from beginning, install by this guide: https://computingforgeeks.com/how-to-install-podman-on-debian/

>  I was going to try and install podman manually using this guide, although I wanted to first troubleshoot why I couldn't find the package in the first place.

You have to install podman manually because there is no package
That's grand thanks for that. Out of curiosity why is podman not available for Buster? Bullseye isn't yet stable?
No one from the community has packaged it.
Ok. I'll build it with the article anyway, thanks very much. I was unsure as to whether it was compatible with my system or not based on the fact that it didn't seem available.
